title: cedrus libani subsp. stenocoma
intro: Cedrus libani subsp. stenocoma, also known as the Atlas cedar, is a subspecies of evergreen coniferous tree native to North Africa and the Iberian Peninsula. The Atlas cedar has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, with its wood and resin possessing anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Cedrus libani subsp. stenocoma include reducing anxiety and stress, improving respiratory health, and treating infections due to its antiseptic properties. Herbal preparations of the Atlas cedar often involve infusions, decoctions, or essential oils extracted from its wood or resin, which can be used to create remedies for various ailments, such as skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues.
